summary: A permanent UN Security Council member, nuclear power, and former global empire whose influence now rests on finance (The City), intelligence (Five Eyes), and the 'special relationship' with the US.

history.detail: First limits on royal power; foundation of constitutional governance.
England and Scotland united as Great Britain.
Thirteen colonies lost; empire pivots to Asia and Africa.
Napoleon defeated; Britain becomes dominant world power.
Height of British Empire — one-quarter of world's land mass.
Over 700,000 British dead; empire begins to weaken.
Churchill's Britain stands alone in 1940 before US entry.
US replaces UK as anchor of the global financial system.
NHS, national insurance — Labour government reforms.
UK humiliated by US; signals end of independent great power status.
Privatisation, financial deregulation, weakening of unions.
War with Argentina over Falkland Islands; UK wins.
Peace deal ends decades of violence in Northern Ireland.
52% vote to leave EU; years of political upheaval follow.
UK exits single market and customs union.

finance.currency_role: GBP — formerly world reserve currency, now major trading currency
finance.central_bank: Bank of England (1694) — world's second oldest central bank
leverage_tools: The City of London (global finance hub)
Crown Dependencies as offshore tax havens

resources.oil_gas: North Sea oil (declining); still significant producer
resources.nuclear: Trident nuclear submarine fleet; civil nuclear programme

follow_the_money.detail: Crown Dependencies (Jersey, Cayman) hold trillions in offshore wealth
Major arms exporter — Saudi Arabia is top customer
£12B/yr aid used as soft power and trade facilitation

theories_disputed.detail: Official verdict: unlawful killing in car crash; conspiracy theories persist.
Snowden revelations 2013 confirmed GCHQ bulk collection via Tempora.
Chilcot Inquiry 2016 confirmed intelligence was manipulated for Iraq War.

connections.detail: Five Eyes founder; 'special relationship' since WWII
Cold War legacy; Ukraine conflict sanctions
Arms sales, oil imports, sovereign wealth investment in London
